book_claims.claim: Whole-grain cracker used as a side in the Longevity Diet.

supplementary_research.finding: Spelt contains higher levels of riboflavin, zinc, and copper than modern wheat; its gluten structure is more water-soluble and may be better tolerated by some gluten-sensitive individuals.
Whole spelt consumption improves gut microbiota composition, increasing Bifidobacterium and Lactobacillus populations due to its resistant starch and arabinoxylan content.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Fermented by gut microbiota to short-chain fatty acids; selectively feeds beneficial Bifidobacterium
Higher than modern wheat; phytate content reduced in whole-grain fermented products
Retained in whole grain; supports mitochondrial energy metabolism and antioxidant recycling

synergies.description: Chickpea protein and spelt fiber create complete amino acid profile and combined prebiotic effect
Olive oil polyphenols reduce the modest glycemic response of spelt starch
Pairing spelt crackers with legume-based dips provides complementary fiber and complete plant protein
